entities.name: Moonshot Project
entities.context: High-difficulty project with uncertain outcomes that expands capability even in failure

entities.name: Slam Dunk
entities.context: Guaranteed victory that reinforces existing competencies but does not expand the toolkit

entities.name: Developmental Value
entities.context: Growth and capability expansion that creates higher long-term ceiling

entities.name: Strategic Value
entities.context: Immediate financial or objective success from a project

entities.name: Stress and Stretch
entities.context: Mechanism through which growth occurs in a system

entities.name: Capacity Building
entities.context: Building persistent assets (skills, resilience) that dictate scale of future attempts

entities.name: Resilience
entities.context: Asset that persists after project ends, built through moonshot attempts

relationships.context: Moonshots provide developmental value even when resulting in objective failure
Slam dunks provide guaranteed strategic/financial value
Moonshots force acquisition of new skills, testing limits, building resilience
Growth is a function of stress and stretch in a system
Founders should alternate between slam dunks (capital/confidence) and moonshots (prevent stagnation)
Capacity building dictates the scale of the next attempt's potential payout
